Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Given a min heap tree with n elements, find the time taken to get the median element of these n elements using the most efficient technique?
[The algorithm can use auxiliary space of Ο(n)].

Correct answer: A
Solution

(c)
Perform an inorder traversal and store all the elements of the min heap tree in an array.
Now, find the median of this unsorted array using the median of medians algorithm which takes Ο(n) time in worst case.

Q18 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : SMA* gives the optimal solution if reachable; otherwise, it returns the best reachable optimal solution.
S2 : Greedy Best-First Search is incomplete even if the search space is finite.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• SMA* is designed to find an optimal solution if one exists; if the optimal solution is not reachable, it returns the best reachable solution. So, S1 is correct.
• Greedy Best-First Search can get stuck in local optima and may fail to find a solution even in finite search spaces, making it incomplete. So, S2 is correct.

Q19 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the relation R(A, B, C, D, E, F) holds following FD’s
AB → C, C → D, D → E, E → F, F → B
What will be the total number of candidate keys presents in above relation ?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
(AB)+ = A B C D E F
(AC)+ = C D E A F B
(AD)+ = D E A F B C
(AF)+ = A F B C D E
(AE)+ = A E F B C D
There are 5 candidate keys are present for given relation.

Q20 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?
S1 : Binary search on array take less time than binary search on linked list.
S2 : Merge sort on array has more space complexity than merge sort on linked list.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Binary search on array take Ο(log (n)) time because random access is possible on array but binary search on linked list take Ο(n) time because random access is not possible. Merge sort on array has more space complexity because it is outplace (i.e. take extra space) but merge sort on linked list take less space because it is inplace (i.e. not take extra space).

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Decision trees use mean absolute error to split nodes in regression tasks.
S2 : The Naive Bayes algorithm assumes all features are dependent given the class label.
Which of the above statements is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Decision trees typically use the Mean Squared Error (MSE) or other criteria like Mean Absolute Error (MAE) for regression tasks, but the common practice is to use MSE for splitting. So, S1 is incorrect.
• The Naive Bayes algorithm indeed assumes that all features are independent given the class label. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q25 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Systematic sampling has higher accuracy than simple random sampling.
S2 : PCA is used to classify data based on pre-defined labels.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Systematic sampling can provide higher accuracy than simple random sampling in certain contexts, particularly when the population has a regular pattern. However, it’s essential to note that the effectiveness can vary based on the population’s structure. So, S1 is correct.
• PCA is used for dimensionality reduction by transforming the data into a new coordinate system based on variance, but it does not classify data based on pre-defined labels. It can aid classification tasks but does not perform classification itself. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q30 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : The snowflake schema normalizes the dimension tables but keeps the fact tables unchanged.
S2 : The snowflake schema results in a more complex structure with multiple related dimension tables.
S3 : The snowflake schema requires less joins during query execution compared to the star schema.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Dimension tables are split into multiple related tables to reduce redundancy, while fact tables typically remain unchanged. So, S1 is correct.
• Normalization leads to a more intricate design, requiring multiple tables for each dimension. So, S2 is correct.
• More joins are necessary to retrieve data due to the normalization of dimension tables. So, S3 is incorrect.

Q31 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Gradient Descent can converge to a local minimum for non-convex functions, which may not be global.
S2 : Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) updates weights individually for each training instance.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Gradient Descent can converge to a local minimum for non-convex functions, which may not be the global minimum. So, S1 is correct.
• Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD) updates the weights for each training instance individually, using only one sample (or a small batch) at a time to compute the gradient. So, S2 is correct.

Q39 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider relation R(A, B, C, D, E, F, H) and the following functional dependencies:

Which of the following is minimal cover of the given functional dependencies?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Checking BD → A, B+ = B, D+ = D, hence BD → A is essential
Checking HD → C, HD → E
H+ = HFDCE, hence it can be decomposed to H → C, H → E so, the dependencies remained are
H → F, H → D, E → D, H → C, H → E, BD → A
Now, {H → E, E → D} by transitive rule H → D can be obtained, hence minimal cover is H → F,
E → D, H → C, H → E, BD → A.

Q50 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

In SVMs, the dual formulation of the optimization problem is used to:
(a) Directly solve the optimization problem with the original feature space.
(b) Simplify the computation by working in a higher dimensional implicitly.
(c) Reduces computational complexity by avoiding the Kernel function.
(d) Allows efficient use of Kernel functions by converting the problem into a quadratic programming problem.
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, D
Solution

(b, d)
• The dual formulation does not solve the problem in the original feature space; it typically transforms the problem into a higher-dimensional space where the solution is found. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• The dual formulation enables the use of kernel functions, allowing computations in a higherdimensional feature space without explicitly mapping data points to that space. So, option (b) is correct.
• The dual formulation does not reduce computational complexity by avoiding the Kernel function; instead, it allows the Kernel function to be used efficiently. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• The dual formulation converts the optimization problem into a quadratic programming problem, which allows for efficient use of Kernel functions. So, option (d) is correct.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• Increasing the regularization coefficient in Lasso regression raises model bias by penalizing coefficient sizes, leading to simpler models. So, option (a) is correct.
• Lasso regression can drive some coefficients to zero for feature selection, but not all coefficients will necessarily be zero. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Bagging reduces variance by averaging predictions from multiple models trained on different data subsets, improving stability. So, option (c) is correct.
• Pruning a decision tree reduces overfitting by removing less significant branches, enhancing generalization. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q60 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) In a weighed undirected graph G = (V, E, ω), breath first search from a vertex S find single source shortest path from S (via parent node) in Ο(V + E) time.
(b) Dijkastra’s shortest path algorithm may relax an edge more than once in a graph with a cycle.
(c) TC to build max heap of n elements is θ(n).
(d) None of these
Which of the above statements is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• In a weighed undirected graph G = (V, E), breath first search from a vertex S find single source shortest path from S (via parent node) in Ο(V + E) time.
• Dijkastra’s algorithm always visits each node at once this is why it produces an incorrect result in the presence of negative weight edges.
• Inserting n elements in empty binary heap takes Ο(n) time because build heap method take Ο(n).

Q65 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) A* algorithm performs better with a Fibonacci heap than with a binary heap.
(b) A* Search Algorithm always produces the shortest path.
(c) A* search is faster than Dijkstra’s algorithm, regardless of the heuristic.
(d) A* search cannot handle graphs with negative weights.
Which of the above statements is/are true?

Correct answer: A, D
Solution

(a, d)
• A* can perform better with a Fibonacci heap due to its better amortized time complexity for decrease-key operations. So, option (a) is correct.
• A* Search does not always produce the shortest path unless the heuristic is admissible (does not overestimate the cost). So, option (b) is incorrect.
• A* is not guaranteed to be faster than Dijkstra’s algorithm; it depends on the quality of the heuristic. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• A* cannot handle graphs with negative edge weights unless a different algorithm, like Bellman- Ford, is used. So, option (d) is correct.
